stories.title: US and Iran Agree to Halt Attacks Ahead of Talks
stories.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce. Bloomberg's Wendy Benjaminson breaks down the latest developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Why brokers believe China’s AI-driven stock run will find fresh legs
stories.summary: Chinese stocks may extend their gains in the second half of the year as resilient exports and recovering producer prices support economic growth while disruption from high oil prices fades, according to brokerages at home and abroad. Average full-year earnings growth for mainland-listed companies could accelerate to 10 per cent on the strength of the economy, while households could channel 2 trill

stories.title: Iran talks on hold after fighting breaks out and Trump once again threatens annihilation
stories.summary: Iran says it targeted U.S. military facilities in Kuwait and Bahrain following U.S. strikes on targets in and around the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Will China’s new northern tech bases in Hebei and Tianjin spark local housing demand?
stories.summary: Chinese technology companies establishing new headquarters and offices in northern cities around Beijing are expected to boost local rental demand, but analysts say home sales growth will be far milder than the surges delivered by traditional tech firms in previous years. Local governments hoping that red-hot artificial intelligence or chip companies setting up headquarters or research and develop

stories.title: Everything tied to the data center is suddenly suspect. Can Big Tech fix it?
stories.summary: The hyperscalers are just beginning to figure out the consequences of the AI arms race, and now everyone seems to be against them.

stories.title: Oil Pares Early Gains as US, Iran Halt Attacks After Flare-Up
stories.summary: Oil pared early gains after the US and Iran agreed to stop attacking each other, following flare-ups over the weekend that saw an oil supertanker hit near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Chinese Spies, Smuggled Drugs Fuel Takaichi’s Security Push
stories.summary: Chinese spies stealing Japanese industrial secrets in boardrooms. Chip smugglers ferrying Nvidia’s prized artificial intelligence semiconductors via Japan. Drug gangs quietly slipping fentanyl across Japan’s borders to a US opioid crisis.

stories.title: Private Credit Quietly Backs the Craze Propping Up US Consumers
stories.summary: Private credit companies are increasingly backing the "buy now, pay later" craze that's propping up US consumers. This trend is throwing up red flags for credit raters, former regulatory chiefs and others on guard for potential risks. René Ismail explains (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: China: Tech Growth Distorts Energy Demand Forecasts
stories.summary: China faces greater uncertainty in forecasting energy demand as structural changes in the economy and the rapid expansion of new industries reshape consumption patterns, according to a top government official. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Gold Declines as Fresh US-Iran Tension Fans Inflation Concerns
stories.summary: Gold declined to near $4,000 an ounce after the US and Iran traded attacks in the Persian Gulf, straining a ceasefire that had last week seen energy prices fall to pre-war levels and tempered expectations for an interest-rate hike.

stories.title: Sovereign investors with US$29 trillion pivot to energy assets, flag dollar fears
stories.summary: Sovereign wealth funds and central banks managing US$29 trillion in assets are turning to energy assets, and raising concerns about the dollar, in a portfolio reassessment driven by unprecedented geopolitical shifts, according to a survey ‌published on Monday by independent global investment management firm Invesco. The survey of 90 sovereign wealth funds and 54 central banks showed an increasing

stories.title: US Stock Futures Rise, Oil Pares Climb on Iran: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US equity-index futures rose after reports the US and Iran backed away from a fresh escalation of their conflict, easing concerns over the fragile ceasefire underpinning peace talks.

stories.title: Russia Ready to Continue Discussing Ukraine With US, Putin Says
stories.summary: The Kremlin expects US negotiators Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to travel to Moscow to continue talks on ending the war in Ukraine once Washington is no longer preoccupied with the conflict with Iran, Russian President Vladimir Putin said in an interview with state television.

stories.title: Aramco helicopter crashes at Saudi port
stories.summary: At least 14 killed in Ras Tanura incident just hours after US-Iran strikes threaten fragile deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Trump’s U-Turn on Iran Sanctions Would Unravel Decades of Curbs
stories.summary: The Trump administration’s effort to unwind decades of sanctions as part of a deal to end the war with Iran has created a head-spinning situation for governments, banks and other companies as they contemplate a shifting patchwork of new permissions and old restrictions.

stories.title: AI Startup Firmus to Build Indonesia Data Center With Nvidia
stories.summary: Firmus Technologies Pty Ltd. will build its first data center project in Indonesia as part of a partnership with US chip giant Nvidia Corp., which the Australian artificial intelligence infrastructure firm said is expected to win it as much as $30 billion in committed offtake agreements in its first six years.

stories.title: Oil jumps back above $70 as fresh U.S.-Iran clashes reignite Middle East supply fears
stories.summary: Oil edged higher on Monday after renewed military strikes between the U.S. and Iran reignited concerns over crude supplies from the Middle East.

stories.title: CNBC Daily Open: Another weekend escalation in Iran, another market shrug
stories.summary: Tensions in the Middle East flared again over the weekend, but markets appeared largely unfazed, with investors instead turning to a tech recovery rally.

stories.title: Kent on Reserve Bank of Australia's New Framework
stories.summary: Australian central bank Assistant Governor Chris Kent said the institution will be better prepared to respond to the next crisis it faces following a review of alternative monetary policy tools. In a speech in Sydney, Kent, who oversees financial markets at the Reserve Bank, said the cash rate target remains the primary and preferred instrument, but additional tools can play an important role duri

stories.title: Philippines Sees Slower Growth, Weak Peso Beyond Marcos’ Term
stories.summary: The Philippines has cut its economic growth targets and sees a weaker peso beyond the end of the term of President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. in 2028 amid headwinds such as the Middle East tensions and an intense El Niño weather event.

stories.title: RBA Will Be Better Prepared to Handle Next Crisis, Kent Says
stories.summary: Australian central bank Assistant Governor Chris Kent said the institution will be better prepared to respond to the next crisis it faces following a review of alternative monetary policy tools.

stories.title: Sovereign funds move from public markets to private to ride AI wave
stories.summary: High concentration in stock markets and national security concerns send SWFs to private credit and infrastructure

stories.title: Wall Street Is Abandoning Bets on a Stronger Euro
stories.summary: Wall Street banks are capitulating on bets for a stronger euro, as markets see the US outpacing Europe on interest-rate hikes for the rest of this year.

stories.title: Prabowo Risks Prompt Global Banks to Pull Cash Out of Indonesia
stories.summary: The three biggest foreign banks in Indonesia have shipped around $640 million of their earnings out of Southeast Asia’s largest economy since 2024 as they pare exposure amid President Prabowo Subianto’s increasingly state-focused economic policies.

stories.title: China, India See Top Firms Lose Market Cap Share in AI Lag
stories.summary: China, India, and Hong Kong have emerged as the only major stock markets worldwide where top companies account for a smaller share of market capitalization than a year ago, underscoring their lag in the global AI race.

stories.title: Animation’s AI Reckoning: Filmmakers Say They Can Make Movies for 90% Less
stories.summary: Some animators believe AI will make films better. Others believe it will bring costs down.

stories.title: Putin says Russia faces fuel shortages as Ukrainian drones strike refineries
stories.summary: President’s comments are first admission that Kyiv’s long-range attacks have dented production

stories.title: BlueBay Sees Near-Term Risk in Japan AI Stocks Followed by Rally
stories.summary: RBC BlueBay Asset Management remains bullish on Japanese AI-related stocks, expecting the rally to extend into 2027, while trimming near-term risk ahead of a potential slowdown in July and August.

stories.title: Kennedy Center Honors Bill Maher With Its Own Future Unknown
stories.summary: In what was once expected to be its last major event before a two-year closure, the Kennedy Center on Sunday will award Bill Maher the Mark Twain Prize for Humor, an annual event that is taking place even as questions swirl about the future of the arts institution President Donald Trump took over at the start of his second term.

stories.title: UK Plans ‘Hybrid’ Warship to Replace Aging Destroyer Fleet
stories.summary: Britain has scrapped plans to replace its aging warships with a new destroyer and will instead procure at least six so-called common combat vessels as it seeks to prepare the military for modern warfare.

stories.summary: Prime minister-in-waiting will also pledge to go further on devolution as scrutiny grows over his likely policies

stories.title: Grim legacy of ‘Chavismo’ hinders Venezuela’s earthquake response
stories.summary: Socialist regime’s US-backed leader is facing a crisis as anger mounts over inadequate rescue efforts

stories.summary: Emirates could have highest expense because of reliance on long-haul flights, MSCI Carbon Markets says

stories.title: New Zealand Dollar Faces Tough Third Quarter on Growth Headwinds
stories.summary: New Zealand’s dollar is facing a subdued third quarter as a resurgent greenback and the lingering effects of the Iran war’s energy shock drag on the domestic recovery.

stories.title: Bond Heavyweights Target a Market Sweet Spot for New Warsh Era
stories.summary: Some of the biggest bond managers are zeroing in on one specific area of the market as the best place to ride out the early days of the Kevin Warsh era.

stories.title: Trump Shifts Messaging from Iran to Economy
stories.summary: Bloomberg News White House Correspondent Jeff Mason joins Bloomberg This Weekend and explains that President Donald Trump is increasingly shifting his public message from the conflict with Iran back to the economy, highlighting lower oil and gasoline prices as the White House seeks to refocus on voters' economic concerns ahead of the midterms. And yet, a recent report has come out that a missed in

stories.title: Hawkish Fed Throws Down Challenge for Emerging-Market Bond Rally
stories.summary: Just when it looked like emerging-market bonds would catch a break from falling energy prices, along came Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh to spoil the party.

stories.title: Faster AI, lower costs: DSpark eases inference bottlenecks and chip strain, says DeepSeek
stories.summary: Chinese artificial intelligence start-up DeepSeek has rolled out a major upgrade to its flagship V4 model aimed at sharply accelerating AI response generation, as competition among Chinese developers increasingly shifts to reducing serving costs and enhancing user experience. DeepSeek, by adopting what it called a speculative decoding framework, DSpark, said it increased per-user response speeds b

stories.title: Google Caps Meta’s Use of Gemini AI, Financial Times Reports
stories.summary: Google has placed limits on Meta Platforms Inc.’s use of its Gemini artificial intelligence models because it could not provide as much computing capacity as the social media company wanted, according to the Financial Times.

stories.title: Ukraine Targets Russian Refineries in Fresh Drone Strikes
stories.summary: Ukraine struck the Slavyansk-na-Kubani oil refinery in Russia’s Krasnodar region and “reached” a refinery in the Yaroslavl region overnight, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on X, calling the attacks part of Kyiv’s “long-range sanctions” campaign against Moscow.

stories.title: How the AI boom exposes investors to risk, while a downturn could see a sharp crash: BIS
stories.summary: As the global financial system struggles to keep pace with the artificial intelligence investment boom and capital flows surge through loosely regulated, non-bank channels, a key international organisation has warned that an AI downturn could develop into a sharper, faster crash than a traditional banking crisis. stories.summary: The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce.

stories.title: Williams Said to Near $5.5 Billion Deal for Momentum Midstream
stories.summary: Williams Cos. is in advanced talks to acquire rival natural gas pipeline operator Momentum Midstream for about $5.5 billion in what would be one of its largest deals ever, people familiar with the matter said.

stories.title: High Schools Emphasizing Personal Finance Education
stories.summary: 39 US states now require students to complete a personal finance course to graduate, up from just one state in 1998. Senior Advocacy Director for the Council for Economic Education Leslie Finnan explains that students who take these courses tend to make better financial decisions, have higher credit scores, and manage student loans more responsibly. stories.title: Supreme Court Leaves Trump’s Fed, Citizenship Gambits for Last
stories.summary: President Donald Trump is on the brink of learning whether the US Supreme Court will bless two of his most audacious gambits, his bids to oust a Federal Reserve governor and roll back automatic birthright citizenship.

stories.title: Can USMCA Survive Trump’s Second Term?
stories.summary: As Canada, Mexico, and the United States begin reviewing the USMCA trade pact, former diplomats and trade negotiators say the stakes extend far beyond tariffs. stories.title: Apple’s Sweeping Price Increases Bring Home the Costs of the AI Era
stories.summary: The latest on the smart glasses competition.

stories.title: Leverage That Fueled US Stock Rally Becomes a Growing Concern
stories.summary: The leverage that helped fuel the US stock rally is now becoming an increasing source of unease.

stories.title: Supreme Court Faces High-Stakes Week
stories.summary: Bloomberg News Supreme Court reporter Greg Stohr joins Bloomberg This Weekend and says the justices are expected to issue rulings this week in two closely watched cases involving President Donald Trump: his attempt to remove Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook and his executive order restricting birthright citizenship. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: If you think China needs to dethrone U.S. dollar, you don't understand how it is waging global currency war
stories.summary: It's misguided to focus on China's renminbi replacing the U.S. dollar. Beijing is already successfully reducing dependence on a dollar-centric global system.

stories.title: Strategist Sees WTI Falling to $40 a Barrel
stories.summary: Rising oil prices are accelerating a supply glut in the Western Hemisphere, Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Commodity Strategist Mike McGlone argues that growing production and improving technology could drive WTI crude down to $40 a barrel. stories.title: Fmr. Deputy National Security Advisor Warns Trump Rhetoric Risks Iran Talks
stories.summary: While the Iran ceasefire remains intact despite recent exchanges of fire, Former Deputy National Security Advisor Ben Rhodes warned it could unravel if tit-for-tat strikes escalate or hardliners on either side gain the upper hand. stories.title: Why the US Needs its Neighbors for Energy
stories.summary: Unlike autos or agriculture, North America’s energy trade does not function as a single, integrated three-country system. Instead, it operates through two powerful bilateral relationships: Canadian oil flowing south into US refineries and American natural gas powering Mexico’s economy. stories.title: OpenAI Hires Uber’s Prabhjeet Singh to Lead India Operations
stories.summary: OpenAI appointed Uber Technologies Inc.’s former India and South Asia President Prabhjeet Singh as its managing director for the country, deepening its investment in one of its fastest-growing markets.

stories.title: As AI pushes data centres to breaking point, some Chinese chipmakers bet on SiC
stories.summary: As the global artificial intelligence (AI) boom puts intense pressure on data centre energy grids, some Chinese chipmakers are betting on highly efficient silicon carbide (SiC) semiconductors to help solve the technology sector’s power problem. stories.summary: Investors are increasingly rolling money from 401(k) plans to IRAs. Some observers fear they're exposed to poor investment advice.

stories.summary: The pace of onshore turbine installations is set to slow until 2030 as the renewables sector faces a myriad of headwinds.

stories.summary: A small plane that crashed into Beijing’s tallest skyscraper on Friday has exposed a vulnerability in the Chinese capital’s controlled airspace, prompting questions over safeguards around politically sensitive locations.
